## [03:02 - 04:32]

those hopes and dreams. You want to take away those fears and frustrations. And so that's what you want to do in your copy. Now, when you're putting together your page, I'm an old school, I love copy. I love convincing people with words, whether it's video, whether it's audio, whether it's just good old text on the page. So I'm a big fan of that, but one of the things you got to think about in your squeeze page is your design. And the markets I've worked in, very simple design tends to work really well. A lot of the markets that I've worked in, now if you have something on like home decorating or graphic design or fashion, things that people are more visual and are weighing into the design element, then your page has to look better and better. For most markets, simple squeeze pages work very well. And no matter what, in your squeeze page you want it to be very obvious what they need to do to carry on. You want to be very obvious about communicating the benefits and then put your email address in here and push the button. You want to be very, very clear about that and you want to give them instructions. You want to tell them to opt in. You don't want them to have to guess at what they do next. So if you give people instructions, they tend to follow those instructions.
